summary: Round 9 finished at 4 from 8, with a rough Friday and Saturday softened by a strong 3 from 3 Sunday that stopped the week from turning into a total washout.

sources.label: Friday Night Lights: Bulldogs v Cowboys; Dolphins v Storm

scoreboard.note: Gold Coast never really got the jump they needed and Canberra made the early upset call look wrong.
North Queensland stormed back to win and the Bulldogs charity play missed despite looking like the cleaner pre-game read.
The Warriors handled Parramatta cleanly and validated one of the steadier reads on the card.
Melbourne fell 26-10 at Suncorp and their losing streak has now stretched to seven straight.
The Broncos returns did not translate into the win, and the Roosters made that gamble look expensive.
Newcastle's returning cavalry mattered and the Knights became the first real Sunday rescue leg.
Cronulla crushed the Tigers and gave the board the cleanest result of the round.
Penrith edged Manly late and completed the 3 from 3 Sunday salvage job.

liveAlerts.text: Round 9 closes at 4 from 8, which is rough but much better than the 1 from 5 hole after Saturday.
The Mark Hughes Foundation ledger stays at 2-2 and -$3.50 after the Bulldogs charity miss.
The clean 3 from 3 on Sunday matters because it stopped the round from becoming a complete write-off.
Storm have now lost seven straight and remain one of the clearest no-trust teams in the comp.
Knights, Sharks and Panthers were the backbone of the Sunday rescue and deserve some credit going into Round 10 prep.
The next step is a calmer Round 10 rebuild, not overcorrecting off one volatile week.
